title: Render workspace/tab badge (D3) + collapsible per-workspace grouping (D4/D6/D7/D8) in switcher.ts
action: Extract a pure, exported function (e.g. groupByWorkspace(rows: AgentRow[]): WorkspaceGroup[], WorkspaceGroup = {workspace_id, workspace_label, workspace_status, rows: AgentRow[]}) from web/src/views/switcher.ts. Groups are sorted alphabetically by workspace_label (per D7); rows within a group keep their original relative order from the input array (per D7, no new sort). Per D3: render each agent row's tab_label as a lighter sub-caption always, regardless of workspace count (empty tab_label renders as nothing, never the literal string 'undefined'). Per D3: render a small workspace badge only when groupByWorkspace(rows).length > 1 (more than one distinct workspace_id present in the current list) - when exactly 1 group, skip both the workspace badge and the section-grouping wrapper entirely, keeping today's flat <ul><li> markup unchanged except for the added tab sub-caption. Per D4/D8: when >1 group, wrap the list in one <section> per workspace, each with a header showing workspace_label plus a status badge for workspace_status (reuse the existing .status-badge/.status-dot CSS classes and status-<value> modifier convention already used for per-agent status, do not invent a parallel styling system), all sections expanded by default. Per D6: each section header has a collapse/expand toggle whose state lives in a local in-memory object/Set scoped to this render call, never written to localStorage/sessionStorage - a page reload always starts fully expanded again. Do not modify the existing pull-to-refresh/touchstart/touchmove/touchend handlers on #switcher-body (confirmed unrelated to list markup by reading the file - they read body.scrollTop, not the list's DOM structure). Add unit tests to web/test/switcher.test.ts for groupByWorkspace covering: empty array, exactly 1 workspace_id, and 2+ distinct workspace_ids (alphabetical ordering asserted), following the existing pure-helper test pattern already used in web/test/terminal.test.ts for stripAnsiLen.

truths: With exactly 1 distinct workspace_id among the current agent rows, no workspace badge and no section grouping render - the list stays a flat <ul><li> matching today's markup, with the new tab sub-caption added

With more than 1 distinct workspace_id, the list renders one collapsible <section> per workspace, sections sorted alphabetically by workspace_label, each header showing workspace_label and a status badge reflecting workspace_status
Every agent row displays its tab_label as a sub-caption regardless of workspace count
All sections render expanded by default; toggling a section's header collapses/expands only that section; collapse state does not persist across a fresh render/reload

groupByWorkspace([]) returns an empty array; groupByWorkspace with 1 distinct workspace_id returns exactly 1 group; groupByWorkspace with 2+ distinct workspace_ids returns groups sorted alphabetically by workspace_label

artifacts.substantive: exports groupByWorkspace (or equivalently-purposed pure function), renderList branches on group count per D3/D4, collapse toggle implemented per D6, no TODO/stub
new rules for the workspace badge and section header, reusing existing design tokens (--radius-full, status-badge pattern) rather than duplicating values
unit tests asserting groupByWorkspace's 0/1/2+ workspace boundary behavior and alphabetical group ordering

key_links: renderList in switcher.ts calls groupByWorkspace and branches its rendering path on the result's length, not a separately maintained count
prohibitions: Do not modify the pull-to-refresh/touchstart/touchmove/touchend event handlers
Do not persist collapse state to localStorage or sessionStorage
Do not change the single-workspace rendering path's existing card markup beyond adding the tab sub-caption
Do not add a new sort for rows within a workspace group - preserve the input array's existing order

verify: cd web && npm run typecheck && npm run test -- --run

trace.outcome: Extracted pure exported groupByWorkspace() helper; switcher.ts renderList now branches to per-workspace collapsible <section>s (D4/D6/D7/D8) when >1 distinct workspace_id else keeps today's flat markup (D3), tab_label sub-caption always shown; added web/test/switcher.test.ts covering 0/1/2+ workspace boundary + alphabetical group order.

  "tests_added_or_changed": ["web/test/switcher.test.ts (new, 3 cases: empty array, 1 workspace_id, 2+ workspace_ids sorted alphabetically)"],
